revision.text:
[[de:Heri-ab wia]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = D2-W7-P3 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Heri-ab wia
''Ḥrj-3b wj3''
''{{ill|Khnum|en|Khnum|qid=Q183097|lt=The|1=Khnum}} (from {{ill|Elephantine|ja|Elephantine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elephantine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the middle of the barque'' }} '''Heri-ab wia''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Egyptian decan|ja|古代エジプトのデカン|lt=Decan|lt_ja=Dekans|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian decan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|ja|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|lt=Dekan-stars|lt_ja=Dekan-Sterne|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most conspicuous {{ill|star|ja|星|lt=star|lt_ja=Stern|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/star|1=星|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1308715}} could be identified as the bluish-white {{ill|Double star|en|Double star|qid=Q13890|lt=double star|1=Double star}} {{ill|Sigma Sagittarii|en|Sigma Sagittarii|qid=Q14036|lt=Nunki|1=Sigma Sagittarii}} in the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|qid=Q8928|lt=constellation|1=Constellation}} {{ill|Sagittarius|ja|射手座|lt=Sagittarius|lt_ja=Schütze|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagittarius|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to いて座|1=Sagittarius (constellation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8866}}. In the decanal lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|ja|ナットブック#ファラオ時代|lt=Sethos script|lt_ja=Sethos-Schrift|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Heri-ab wia represented the 14th decan in the body of {{ill|Nut (goddess)|en|Nut (goddess)|qid=Q165496|lt=Nut|1=Nut (goddess)}}. The heliacal rising was scheduled for the {{ill|16th Achet I|ja|アチェット#年代|lt=16th Achet I|lt_ja=16. Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/16th Achet I|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was based on the dating scheme established under {{ill|Sesostris III|ja|セソストリス3世|lt=Sesostris III|lt_ja=Sesostris III.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesostris III|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty|ja|第12王朝|lt=12th Dynasty|lt_ja=12. Dynastie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/12th Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト第12王朝|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q719639}}) in his seventh regnal year. Heri-ab wia belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|ja|羊|lt=Sheep|lt_ja=Schaf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=Sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7368}}. Due to the {{ill|Quenching (fluorescence)|en|Quenching (fluorescence)|qid=Q585536|lt=extinction|1=Quenching (fluorescence)}}, Heri-ab wia is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.1°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 12 to 15 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting, respectively. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|ja|Christian Leitz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christian Leitz|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Ancient Egyptian star clocks''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the course of the stars – The so-called Nutbook''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (among others), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and p. 385. {{translated page|de|Heri-ab wia|version=221308569|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Heri-ab wia]] {{wikidata link|Q1609860}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
